UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user profile in the Veiled Ventures application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user profile.

bigFiveScores.description: The user's Big Five personality scores (stored as a JSON string).

experienceYears.description: The user's years of professional experience.

profileTags.description: Tags associated with the user's profile.

anonymizedProfile.description: The anonymized profile text generated by the AI.

JobListing.title: JobListing
JobListing.description: Represents a job listing in the Veiled Ventures application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the job listing.

idealCandidateMbti.description: The ideal candidate's MBTI personality type for this job.

tags.description: Tags associated with the job listing.

anonymizedDescription.description: The anonymized job description generated by the AI.

Company.title: Company
Company.description: Represents a company listing a job.

hiringManagerMbti.description: The hiring manager's MBTI personality type.

hiringManagerBigFive.description: The hiring manager's Big Five personality scores (stored as a JSON string).

MatchScore.title: MatchScore
MatchScore.description: Represents the match score between a user and a job listing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the match score entry.

score.description: The match score between the user and the job listing.

Message.title: Message
Message.description: Represents a message exchanged between a candidate and an employer.

senderId.description: Reference to UserProfile or Company. (The sender of the message).

receiverId.description: Reference to UserProfile or Company. (The receiver of the message).

Deposit.title: Deposit
Deposit.description: Represents a deposit made by a company for a job listing.

dueDate.description: The date when proof of hire must be submitted.

refundPercentage.description: The percentage of the deposit to be refunded.

proofOfHireSubmitted.description: Indicates whether proof of hire has been submitted.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access their profile.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores job listings. Access control is based on the `companyId` field within each document. Includes `companyId` for authorization independence.

params.name: jobListingId
params.description: The unique identifier for the job listing.

definition.description: Stores company information. Access control may be based on admin roles or company ownership.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the company.

definition.description: Stores match scores between users and job listings. Allows efficient querying of matches. Includes `userProfileId` and `jobListingId` for association.

params.name: matchScoreId
params.description: The unique identifier for the match score.

definition.description: Stores messages within a conversation. Access control is based on the `senderId` and `receiverId` fields, ensuring only participants can access the messages.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the conversation.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the message.

definition.description: Stores deposit information for job listings. Associated with JobListing IDs.

params.name: depositId
params.description: The unique identifier for the deposit.

definition.description: Indicates admin roles. Existence of document implies admin access.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support Veiled Ventures' anonymous job matching platform, emphasizing phased information reveals, AI-driven anonymization, and commitment through deposits and tiered messaging. It adheres to Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, Access Modeling, and Data Clarity principles.

**Authorization Independence**: Achieved through denormalization. For example, job listings contain the company ID, and match scores are stored independently of user profiles and job listings. This avoids the need for `get()` calls in security rules, ensuring atomic operations and simplifying authorization logic.

**Structural Segregation**: Different data types with varying access requirements are stored in separate collections. User profiles, job listings, companies, and match scores are each stored in their own top-level collections.  This segregation simplifies security rules by ensuring a homogeneous security posture within each collection. Messages are further segregated under `/conversations/{conversationId}/messages/{messageId}`.

**Access Modeling**:
*   **User Profiles**: Private data owned by the user, stored under `/users/{userId}/profile`. This leverages path-based ownership.
*   **Job Listings**: Stored under `/jobListings/{jobListingId}`. Access is controlled based on the company that owns the job listing, with the `companyId` used for authorization.
*   **Companies**: Stored under `/companies/{companyId}`.  Administrators can be granted global roles via the `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection.
*   **Match Scores**: Stored under `/matchScores/{matchScoreId}` and associated with both UserProfile and JobListing IDs. This allows for efficient querying of matches for a specific user or job. Because MatchScore references both UserProfile and JobListing the list operation does not require that User has access to those items.
*   **Messages**: Stored in a subcollection under `/conversations/{conversationId}/messages/{messageId}`. The `conversationId` implies a shared context between two users (candidate and employer). Authorization for messages relies on the `senderId` and `receiverId` fields within each message, ensuring that only participants can access the messages.
*   **Deposits:** Stored under `/deposits/{depositId}` and directly associated with JobListing IDs. This enables efficient retrieval of deposit information for a specific job.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters)**: The structure supports secure `list` operations.  For example, listing job listings requires no filtering based on user roles or attributes; the rules simply verify if the user has the appropriate permissions to view the listings. The match score collection structure is designed to enable listing matches for a given user or job listing without requiring access to the underlying user profile or job listing documents. The separation of data into distinct collections, combined with denormalized authorization data, ensures that rules can be written to allow or deny access based on the current user's role and the specific resource being accessed, without relying on complex filtering logic.

**Invariants**: Timestamps are stored within the Message documents to preserve message ordering and history. Ownership of data is enforced through path-based ownership (user profiles) and denormalized fields (companyId in job listings).
